title: Stirring device and method for concentrating macromolecules
abstract: The present invention relates to a stirring device and a centrifuge method for concentrating macromolecules from a solution.
claims:
Claims 1. A sealing cap adapted to a centrifuge tube with a concentrator device, comprising an electric motor, a battery, which is placed in the centrifuge, and a stirring element, for homogenization of a macromolecular solution during the process of being concentrated by means of spinning the concentrator device in a centrifuge, wherein the electric motor is powered by the battery. 2. The sealing cap of claim 1, wherein said electric motor and said stirring element are connected via a rotational core. 3. The sealing cap of claim 1, wherein said stirring element is connected with a magnetic stirrer, which is capable of moving the stirring element. 4. The sealing cap of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein said sealing cap is a screw cap or a plug cap fitting on a centrifuge tube. 5. The sealing cap of claim 4, wherein said screw cap or said plug cap is characterized by a sealed housing, which comprises on one side an on/off switch, on a further side the stirring element and inside of the housing the electric motor. 6. The sealing cap of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein said battery is placed in the sealing cap or is separately placed within another centrifugal inlet, or fixed onto the centre of the centrifuge's rotor, preferably via a magnet or a supporting frame, and connected via wires with the electric motor. 7. The sealing cap of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein said battery is a rechargeable battery. 8. The sealing cap of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the concentrator device comprises at least one membrane and a dead stop, in which a portion of the macromolecular solution remains unfiltered. 9. The sealing cap of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the concentrator device is positioned in the central part of the centrifuge tube. 10. The sealing cap of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the stirring element is a stirring staff having a shape selected from the group comprising, a paddle blade shape, a ribbon blade shape, a turbine vortex blade shape, an umbrella type blade shape, an anchor blade, shape a flat blade turbine tube shape, a spiral propeller blade shape, a high shear homogenizer shape and a coiled spiral shape. 11. The sealing cap of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the stirring element is vertically oriented. 12. The sealing cap of any one of claims 7 to 11, wherein the stirring element reaches into the dead stop of the concentrator device. 13. The sealing cap of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the stirring staff is modular exchangeable, preferably through a plugging mechanism, a magnetic mechanism or a screwing mechanism. 14. Use of the sealing cap of any one of claims 1 to 13 for homogenizing a macromolecular solution during the spinning of a centrifuge tube with a concentrator device. 15. The use of the sealing cap as required by claim 14, wherein said homogenization takes place during the process of concentrating macromolecules from said solution via filtration across a membrane present in the concentrator device. 16. An assembly for homogenization of a macromolecular solution during the process of being concentrated by means of spinning a concentrator device in a centrifuge, comprising a centrifuge tube, a concentrator device, which comprises at least one membrane and a dead stop, in which a portion of the macromolecular solution remains unfiltered, and a sealing cap as defined in any one of claims 1 to 13. 17. The assembly of claim 16, wherein: (i) the concentrator device is positioned in the central part of the centrifuge tube to obtain two chambers, one with an open side and one entirely sealed with the tube and a membrane of the concentrator device; (ii) the stirring element is positioned on one side of the sealing cap; and/or (iii) the sealing cap seals the open side of the centrifuge tube while the stirring element is inside of the centrifuge tube and the on/off switch outside of the centrifuge tube. 18. Use of the assembly of claim 16 or 17 for effectively concentrating macro- molecules from a solution. 19. A method for avoiding membrane fouling during concentrating macromolecules from a solution, comprising spinning a centrifuge tube with a concentrator device, which comprises at least one membrane and a dead stop, in 

 which a portion of the macromolecular solution remains unfiltered, wherein said method comprises the step of stirring said solution during said spinning process. 20. A method for effectively concentrating macromolecules from a solution, comprising spinning a centrifuge tube with a concentrator device which comprises at least one membrane and a dead stop, in which a portion of the macromolecular solution remains unfiltered in a centrifuge, wherein said centrifuge tube is covered by a sealing cap adapted to the centrifuge comprising an electric motor, a battery and a stirring element as defined in any one of claims 1 to 13 and wherein said solution is stirred during the process of being concentrated by means of spinning the concentrator device in said centrifuge. 21. The method of claim 19 or 20, wherein said stirring is performed during (i) the entire process of being concentrated by means of spinning the concentrator device in said centrifuge, (ii) during about 20 to 75% of the time of said spinning process, or (iii) during the last about 25% of the time of said spinning process. 22. The method of any one of claims 19 to 21, wherein said stirring homogenizes said solution to sweep the portion of the solution which remains unfiltered in a dead stop of the concentrator device in a direction towards at least a part of the at least one membrane of said concentrator device. 23. A concentrated macromolecular solution obtainable with the method of any one of claims 19 to 22, which is essentially free of macromolecular aggregates.
